meaning: n.有条纹或方格纹的棉布,洋伞
examples: Gingham fabrics always look fresh and pretty.
The chambermaid in our corridor wears blue checked gingham aprons.
She was handmade, with a painted face, and she wore a gingham dress to conceal her baby bulge.
The attendants will give you a cloth (resembling, in most establishments, an over-sized red gingham tea towel).
Prada's Shetland-wool sweaters and gingham-patterned coats and dresses of stiff wool created a nostalgic feel.
